meta.title: NIX — Character Sheet
meta.ogDescription: Fetchling · Barbarian // Alchemist · Gestalt Level 3 — "This is not your party, Ember."

identity.label: Languages
identity.value: Common, Shadowtongue, Draconic, Necril, D'ziriak, Shae, (1 reserved)

appearance.label: Hair
appearance.value: Neon yellow with a shock of bright pink

appearance.label: Tattoos
appearance.value: Nix's work covers the scarring in large, frantic strokes, redaction rather than decoration. Beneath and at the edges, Ember's original clan markings are still visible: deliberate, elegant, the work of someone with a completely different relationship to their own skin.

appearance.label: Tell
appearance.value: Head occasionally tilts at a slight, genuinely curious angle. Slightly to the left of your soul.

appearance.label: Warning
appearance.value: Goes very, very still just before violence.

abilityScoresNote: Racial adjustments applied: DEX +2, CHA +2, WIS -2

features.name: Rage Power: Intimidating Glare
features.description: Intimidate as move action while raging

features.name: Throw Anything · Swift Alchemy · Brew Potion

classFeaturesNote: Precise Bombs discovery planned for Level 4, following *The Incident.*
skillsNote: Free ranks from Alchemist · 6 pts/level x 3 levels = 18 total · WIS-based skills are inaccessible. Nix does not make Perception, Sense Motive, Heal, or Survival rolls. Keith determines what Nix notices passively. Languages accumulate the same way, absorbed through exposure without deliberate study. Nix may speak or insert words from a language without registering that no one else understands it.

traits.name: Shadow Blending
traits.description: Miss chance in dim light increases to 50%

equipment.name: Chain Shirt
equipment.notes: +4 AC · max DEX +4 · no arcane spell failure for extracts

lore.title: Raventongue
lore.content: God of the lost, forgotten, and unwanted. Patron of orphans and thieves. His holy symbol is a rapier with a winged, swept hilt. His people on the Bound Isles do not ask where you came from.

lore.title: Ember
lore.content:
Ember was the last child born to the **Light Singers**, a clan of fetchling nomads who spent generations traveling west across the Plane of Shadow, forever following the eternally setting sun. The winter Ember was born, every woman in the clan became barren on the same night. The elders knew the prophecy. They did not speak it aloud.

They raised her together instead. Every story the clan had ever carried, every recipe, every song, every memory stretching back further than anyone could trace, poured into her. Ember became a living archive. The last ember of everything the Light Singers had ever been.

Language was part of that inheritance. The Light Singers had traveled far and traded widely, and the elders between them carried a dozen tongues. Ember absorbed them the way she absorbed everything else, not through study but through proximity and repetition, languages settling into her the same way stories did. She did not learn them so much as accumulate them.

She was given clan tattoos as she grew. Deliberate. Elegant. Ceremonial. The markings of someone who knew exactly who they were.

When Ember came of age, the clan stopped walking. They had arrived. A crumbling temple, half consumed by shadow, half blazing with the light of the setting sun. Each elder told her one final story. A story of calamity and renewal. She cannot remember the words. She feels them still.

The last thing Ember saw of home was the faces of everyone she had ever loved, slowly consumed by darkness blacker than death as the portal opened beneath her feet.

lore.title: The Pit
lore.content:
The vampire lords did not take the Light Singers for food. They took them for sport.

The clan was separated, imprisoned, and introduced to the arena one threshold at a time. The fights began with beasts. Then monsters. Then rival clans. Each time Ember survived, she was rewarded, elixirs that produced euphoria unlike anything she had words for. Warmth. Relief. The chemical sensation of having done something *right.*

She began to associate the arena with that feeling. The conditioning was patient and thorough.

The vampires spoke among themselves in Necril. They did not bother to hide it. Ember was not supposed to survive long enough for it to matter. She absorbed it anyway, the same way she absorbed everything, passively, without meaning to. By the time they noticed, it was already inside her.

By the time they put her against members of her own clan, the threshold had already been crossed so many times it barely registered. By the time they put her against her family, her hands were already steady.

Her spouse was last. She does not remember how long the fight lasted. She remembers the expression on their face at the end. Not hatred. Not fear.

*Forgiveness.*

She remembers what the elixir felt like afterward. That is the part that has no shape. That is the wound underneath all the other wounds, the one the tattoos were never going to cover.

lore.title: The Name
lore.content:
When it was over, the Archlord looked at what remained of Ember and gave it a name. *Nix.* A curse word in his mother tongue. Then he pressed a drop of his own blood to their lips, spoke three bindings into it, and cast them into the void between planes.

An experiment. What happens when you take everything from someone, and then take even more?

Nix kept the name. They don't fully know why. Somewhere underneath the chaos, underneath the voices and the conditioning and the arguments, something kept it on purpose. Something that hasn't announced itself as a reason. Something patient and small and not entirely extinguished.

*Ember knows why.*

Nix tells her to shut up about it.

bindings.label: Binding I
bindings.name: You cannot die by your own hand.
bindings.description: The blood crystallizes in Nix's veins if they attempt self-destruction, paralyzing, agonizing, absolute. This is not mercy. The experiment is not finished. Reckless choices are permitted. Suicide is not.

bindings.label: Binding II
bindings.name: You cannot be hidden from him.
bindings.description: The Archlord always knows where Nix is. He has not come to collect them yet. This is its own kind of horror, being watched by something that hasn't decided what to do with you.

bindings.label: Binding III
bindings.name: You hear them all.
bindings.description: The Archlord's blood carries the essence of everyone whose blood has ever mingled with his. Nix hears their voices. All of them. Constantly. Ember is the one Nix gave a name to. Ember is the one Nix argues with out loud. The others have not been named.

lore.title: Nix
lore.content:
Nix emerged from the void onto the Bound Isles. Found Raventongue's people. Survived. Functioned. After a fashion.

The guild came later, not by plan. Nix happened to see a member of the **Blightfather's** clergy speaking to a Delvers Guild recruiter. The Blightfather is known to abhor vampires. Something steered Nix toward that conversation without announcing itself as a reason. Nix signed the contract. They tell themselves it was practical.

*Ember hasn't said anything about it. Which is unusual.*

The guild quartermaster has an informal chalk mark system for when Nix comes in. Everyone is suddenly very busy with paperwork. It's the stillness, mostly. The way Nix goes completely, perfectly calm at the exact moment when a reasonable person would not be calm. The slight tilt of the head.

That, and the arguments with Ember.

Nix does not lie. Not from principle. There is simply no model for the gap between what is true and what is useful to say. Information is transmitted because it is accurate. For the same reason, Nix does not register that others might operate differently. Someone lying to Nix is received as data. It will be acted on, literally and completely, with no cross-referencing against motive. This is not naivety in the soft sense. It is the same absence that makes the WIS score what it is. The filter is not broken. It was never installed.

lore.title: The Ember Problem
lore.content:
Ember is still in there. She remembers everything, every story, every recipe, every face of the clan, every moment in the arena, every reward, every threshold crossed, the expression at the end. She has opinions. She does not stay quiet.

Nix does not open the archive. Nix addresses Ember directly, out loud, when necessary, and considers this perfectly normal. It is not perfectly normal.

Ember is also the loudest voice. She is not the only one.

incidents.text: "No, Ember. The dagger is not a weapon. I don't care what you think."

lore.title: A Note on the Conditioning
lore.content:
Nix uses bombs. Nix has a mutagen. Nix goes very still before violence and very calm during it.

The vampire lords' conditioning did not disappear when Ember became Nix.

*It just got a new name.*

lore.title: Campaign Hooks
hooks: The Archlord is always watching. He hasn't moved yet. Why?
The prophecy the elders spoke at the temple. Nix cannot access it. Ember feels it in her soul. What was it actually about?
The Blightfather's clergy member who was speaking to the recruiter. Coincidence, or was Nix being guided toward the guild deliberately?
Whatever drew the Light Singers west for generations. Was the temple the destination, or a trap laid long before Ember was born?
Tyler's character believes vampires are an abomination. He does not yet know what's sitting next to him at the table.
The voices in the blood. Who are they? What do they want? Are any of them dangerous in their own right?

footer: Nix · Level 3 · Fetchling Barbarian // Alchemist · Gestalt
